commands.review_summary:
Adds `channels.telegram.spooledUpdateHandlerTimeoutMs` to Telegram config schema/types/UI metadata/docs/tests and passes the merged account value into isolated polling sessions.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +24, Docs +3, Generated 0. Total +43 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection reproduces the config gap: current main has an internal spooled handler timeout hook but no accepted public Telegram config key for it. I did not run a live Telegram/runtime reproduction in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(telegram): expose spooled handler timeout config
target.body:
## Summary
- expose `channels.telegram.spooledUpdateHandlerTimeoutMs` with top-level and per-account config support
- pass the configured value into Telegram isolated polling sessions
- document the timeout and regenerate bundled channel config metadata

## Why
Telegram isolated ingress already fails stale claimed spool updates after a handler timeout, but operators could not tune that timeout from `channels.telegram`. A wedged `.processing` file can therefore keep a same-chat lane blocked until the default 25 minute timeout.

## Validation
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` passed locally while preparing the patch. A later rerun in this workspace timed out in Vitest/Rolldown pre-test bundling with repeated plugin timing warnings before any assertion output.
target.author: khang-dogo
